zh.lifeSummary: 當Hawise de la Pole於1291年7月25日出生在英國威爾斯蒙格莫利夏爾時，她的父親Owain ap Griffith Prince Of Upper Powys34歲，母親Joanna Corbet30歲。她於1309年7月26日與Sir John de Cherleton 1st Lord Cherleton在英國英格蘭史洛普爾萊可沃迪結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1353年在英國史洛普爾史福斯波利享年62歲，埋葬在英國史洛普爾史福斯波利史福斯波利 聖 朱利安。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Hawise de la Pole于1291年7月25日出生在英国威尔斯蒙格莫利夏爾时，她的父亲Owain ap Griffith Prince Of Upper Powys34岁，母亲Joanna Corbet30岁。她于1309年7月26日与Sir John de Cherleton 1st Lord Cherleton在英国英格兰史洛普爾萊可沃迪结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1353年在英国史洛普爾史福斯波利享年62岁，埋葬在英国史洛普爾史福斯波利史福斯波利 聖 朱利安。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: la
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Hung, Cuong, Anh, Thanh, Ha, Hoang, Tuan, Chau, Hao, Hien, Hoa, Hue, Nam, Phong, Sinh, Tuong, Min, Tam, Tanh, Thai, Tham, Tinh, Yeong.  Chinese Hong, Hang, Sang, Tong, Chan, Chi, Hwa Ja, Hyung, Kam, Ki Bong, Kin, Man.
Vietnamese: from the Chinese surname 羅, see   Luo   1.
Vietnamese (Lã): from the Chinese surname 呂, see Lu 6 and 3 below.

variants.name: Luo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Jian, Yi, Ping, Yong, Zhen, Feng, Hong, Ming, Jin, Bin, Chen, Hai, Lan, Min, Huan, Hung, Shen, Tian.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 羅, meaning ‘net for catching birds’ or ‘to collect’ in Chinese: (i) from Luo (羅), the name of an ancient state (located in present-day Hubei province). After it was annexed by the state of Chu during the early Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ), people from the state migrated southwards and adopted Luo (羅) as their surname. (ii) from the Po Duo Luo (破多羅) and Chi Luo (叱羅) families from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China, who changed their original surnames to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) from the personal name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅), leader of an ancient Turkic tribe who are believed to have migrated to China during the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ). His descendants inherited his name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅) as their surname and shortened it to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iv) from the name of one of the seven families from the Bandun Man, an ethnic group residing in ancient southwestern China (located mainly in present-day Sichuan province).

nameMeanings.name: Pole
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Leicestershire): variant of   Paul   or   Poole  .
Americanized form of German   Pohle   or   Pohl  .

variants.name: Poles
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Livio, Primo, Santo.
English and Welsh: variant of Pole and Powell + genitival or excrescent -s.
Italian (Friuli and Veneto): probably a derivative of the personal name   Polo  . It is also found in Slovenia.

variants.name: Paul
meanings: English, French, German, Dutch, Swedish, West Indian (mainly Haiti, also e.g. Saint Lucia), and African (mainly Nigeria and Tanzania): from the personal name Paul (from Latin Paulus ‘small’), which has always been popular in Christendom. It was the name adopted by the Pharisee Saul of Tarsus after his conversion to Christianity on the road to Damascus in about AD 34. He was a most energetic missionary to the Gentiles in the Roman Empire, and played a very significant role in establishing Christianity as a major world religion. The name was borne also by numerous other early Christian saints. It is also occasionally borne by Jews; the reasons for this are not clear. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed many cognates from other languages and their patronymics and other derivatives, e.g. Greek Pavlis , Slovenian Pavel and Pavlič (see Pavlic ), Polish Paweł (see Pawel ) and Pawlicki , Assyrian/Chaldean Polous and Polus . In France, this surname is most common in Brittany (see 2 below).
Breton (mainly Finistère): from a Frenchified form of the personal name Paol, Breton form of Paul .
Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Phóil ‘son of Paul’. Compare McFall .

nameMeanings.name: Hawise
meanings: This female name (which has been corrupted down to Avice and Avis) is from Hadewisa, formed from the German name Hedvig, same as Edwig, which Wachter translates 'propugnator felicitatis;' say, 'fortunate soldier.'

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Hawise de la Pole was born on 25 July 1291, in Montgomeryshire, Wales, United Kingdom, her father, Owain ap Griffith Prince Of Upper Powys, was 34 and her mother, Joanna Corbet, was 30. She married Sir John de Cherleton 1st Lord Cherleton on 26 July 1309, in Wrockwardine, Shropshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 2 daughters. She died in 1353, in Shrewsbury, Shropshire, England, at the age of 62, and was buried in Shrewsbury St Julian, Shrewsbury, Shropshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Hawise de la Pole est née le 25 juillet 1291, à Montgomeryshire, Pays de Galles, Royaume-Uni, son père, Owain ap Griffith Prince Of Upper Powys, avait 34 ans et sa mère, Joanna Corbet, avait 30 ans. Elle a épousé Sir John de Cherleton 1st Lord Cherleton le 26 juillet 1309, à Wrockwardine, Shropshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée en 1353, à Shrewsbury, Shropshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 62 ans, et a été enterrée à Shrewsbury St Julian, Shrewsbury, Shropshire, Angleterre.

ja.lifeSummary: Hawise de la Poleが1291年7月25日にイギリスウェールズモントゴメリシュアイアで生まれた時，父Owain ap Griffith Prince Of Upper Powysは34歳，母Joanna Corbetは30歳であった。1309年7月26日にイギリスイギリスシュロプシュアイアウロックワーディンでSir John de Cherleton 1st Lord Cherletonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1353年にイギリスシュロプシュアイアシュリュースブリーで62歳で死去し，イギリスシュロプシュアイアシュリュースブリーシュリュースブリー スト ジュリアンにて埋葬された。

ko.Shrewsbury St Julian, Shrewsbury, Shrops: 영국 슈럽실르 슈레w스버이 슈레w스버이 스트 줄리언
ko.lifeSummary: Hawise de la Pole(이)가 1291년 7월 25일 영국 웨일스 몽고머이실르에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Owain ap Griffith Prince Of Upper Powys(은)는 34세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Joanna Corbet(은)는 30세였다. 그녀는 1309년 7월 26일 영국 잉글랜드 슈럽실르 록워딘에서 Sir John de Cherleton 1st Lord Cherleton(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1353년 영국 슈럽실르 슈레w스버이에서 62세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 슈럽실르 슈레w스버이 슈레w스버이 스트 줄리언에 묻혔다.

Ela casou-se com Sir John de Cherleton 1st Lord Cherleton em 26 de julho de 1309, em Wrockwardine, Shropshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 2 filhas. ru.Shrewsbury St Julian, Shrewsbury, Shrops: шрюсбури ст джулян, шрюсбури, шропшир, Англия
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Hawise de la Pole родилась 25 июля 1291 г. в местности монтгомиришир, Уэльс, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Owain ap Griffith Prince Of Upper Powys было 34 года, а матери, которую звали Joanna Corbet, 30. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir John de Cherleton 1st Lord Cherleton 26 июля 1309 г. в местности рокквардаин, шропшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 2 дочери. Она умерла в 1353 г. в местности шрюсбури, шропшир, Англия в возрасте 62 лет и была похоронена в местности шрюсбури ст джулян, шрюсбури, шропшир, Англия.

Se casó con Sir John de Cherleton 1st Lord Cherleton el 26 de julio de 1309, en Wrockwardine, Shropshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió en 1353, en Shrewsbury, Shropshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 62 años, y fue sepultada en Shrewsbury St Julian, Shrewsbury, Shropshire, Inglaterra.
